steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content:
Left of the blank: Subject noun “Antiquarians”
Right of the blank: Infinitive phrase “to read...” and then the clause with modal verb “might procure”

steps.title: Apply the rule
steps.content:
A participle modifies a noun while a finite verb serves as a main verb.

The participle “hoping” modifies “Antiquarians” while “might procure” is the sentence's main verb.

Any finite verb form (hoped, hope, have hoped) would create two competing verbs without clear coordination.

That matches Choice B — hoping.
